title: Execution agent over-engineering patterns degrading target codebase
author: peteromallet

stage1.summary: Thoroughly documented meta issue from the maintainer with concrete evidence of 7 anti-patterns. The fixes are already substantially implemented: execution_constraints.py in engine/_plan/policy/ encodes all 7 constraints as enforceable rules, and the maintainer's own comment confirms 0.9.6 shipped with these guardrails. The issue should remain open for tracking until all constraint enforcement is verified end-to-end (constraints exist as strings but need to be confirmed they are actually injected into reviewer/triage prompts and tested).
stage1.suggested_fix: Verify that EXECUTION_CONSTRAINTS from execution_constraints.py are injected into all relevant prompt templates (review batch prompts, triage sense-check prompts, stage instruction blocks). Add integration tests confirming constraint text appears in generated prompts. Once verified, close the issue.

stage3.reasoning: The execution anti-pattern guardrails are implemented and now verified in generated prompts. `EXECUTION_CONSTRAINTS` contains the shared constraints, external review prompts include the rendered constraints block, and triage sense-check instructions include the same constraints so agents flag over-engineered cleanup steps before execution.
changes_applied: Verified `EXECUTION_CONSTRAINTS` is injected into external review launch prompts.
Verified triage sense-check instructions include the shared execution constraints.
Added regression tests covering both prompt paths.
